scope.summary: Cluster E complete on the oraclemcp side: the swarm constitution is Charter v2 (eighteen rules), and rules 13-18 each ship a mechanism that settles their question from git or an exit status, wired into the CI lint job.
out_of_scope: The rust-oracledb half of 'both repos': rules 13-18 and the script landed in oraclemcp only, because that checkout belongs to the driver panes. Tracked open as oraclemcp-m02vm, which is why this close claims not-ready.
Enforcement by git hook: every mechanism is a refusal an agent runs, not a locked door. CI runs the selftest and the unbounded-wait lint; it does not run the per-path, per-commit queries.
The workspace gate itself: verified-push requires --gate-cmd and is deliberately unopinionated about which gate you ran.

entry_points.name: scripts/swarm_discipline.sh --selftest
entry_points.trace: 14 assertions, both directions of every mechanism, exit 0: AT-HEAD vs FOREIGN-EDIT (13), proof-vs-close tree_clean (14), a failed gate refusing the push and recording {"status": "fail"} (15), a deadline returning 124 and a fast command untouched (16), tail -f refused while build_lease.sh's deadline-bounded loop passes (16), a split struct field refused and the complete change accepted (17), a delete of a live path refused (18). Wired into .github/workflows/ci.yml as 'swarm discipline (constitution rules 13-17)' alongside `unbounded-wait-lint`, which is clean over the repository's 96 committed shell files.

entry_points.name: python3 scripts/audit_bead_closes.py --template <id> --scope <path>
entry_points.trace: The scaffold that caused the E_TREE_DIRTY close now scopes tree_clean to the bead's claimed paths: on this live tree (35-39 foreign dirty paths) unscoped emits false, a clean scope emits true plus a foreign-dirt note, and a dirty scope emits false plus the paths to commit. `--self-test` PASS; `check_bead_close_evidence.sh` reports 0 hard findings.

entry_points.name: scripts/swarm_discipline.sh stale-delete-check --commit ebac68f
entry_points.trace: Replayed against the session's own stale-index commit it names all five deleted-but-present evidence documents and exits 65 — the mechanism catching the incident that produced it.

limitations: Six of the eight original children (E1-E7 plus the E4 follow-up) were closed by earlier sessions; this close verifies the Charter v2 half it delivered and does not re-audit theirs.
The mechanisms are static and local: no CI run was dispatched from this close, and the ci.yml wiring is verified by reading the file and running both commands locally.
Rule 17's check is static, not a compiler; rule 16's loop heuristic proves the shape of a bound, not that the bound is correct.
